content.Text: [Wed 2026-04-22 14:39 PDT] Create a new interactive tool page `docs/symmetry.html` for GraphVisual - a "Graph Symmetry Explorer" that lets users explore graph automorphisms and symmetry orbits. ## What to build A self-contained HTML page (no external JS deps, uses styles.css for theming) at `docs/symmetry.html` that: 1. **Canvas graph visualization** - nodes/edges drawn on canvas with drag support 2. **Automorphism finder** - brute-force permutation search for small graphs (≤12 nodes) to find all graph automorphisms (permutations that preserve adjacency) 3. **Orbit visualization** - color nodes by their symmetry orbit (nodes in same orbit = same color) 4. **Symmetry group info** - show |Aut(G)| (automorphism group order), number of orbits, orbit sizes, whether graph is vertex-transitive/edge-transitive/asymmetric 5. **8 presets**: Complete K5, Cycle C6, Petersen graph, Path P5, Star S6, Cube Q3, Prism, Asymmetric tree 6. **Text input** - paste adjacency list 7. **Step animation** - animate through automorphisms showing node mapping 8. **Proactive insights** - analyze symmetry properties and provide recommendations (e.g. "High symmetry suggests this graph is good for load balancing", "Asymmetric graph - every node is structurally unique") 9. **JSON export** of automorphisms and orbits ## Style/Pattern Follow the exact same pattern as the other tool pages (independent-set.html, dominating-set.html, etc.): - Same sidebar navigation (copy from independent-set.html) but add a `🔮Symmetry Explorer` entry in the Tools section - Same CSS variable usage (--surface, --bg, --border, --accent, --text, --text-muted, etc.) - Same layout pattern: `.controls-panel` on left (320px), canvas on right - Same control-group, btn, preset-btn, stat-card styling - Dark theme compatible via styles.css - `` ## Also update 1. `docs/index.html` - Add a sidebar link for symmetry.html: `🔮Symmetry Explorer` in the Tools section (add it after the link-predictor entry) ## Algorithm notes For automorphism detection with ≤12 nodes, use a backtracking approach: - For each possible mapping of node 0, try to extend to a full automorphism - Prune: mapped node must have same degree as target - Prune: adjacency must be preserved for already-mapped nodes - This is fast enough for small graphs Orbits: two nodes are in the same orbit if some automorphism maps one to the other. Do NOT add any Java files - this is purely a frontend HTML tool.
I’m aligning the new page with the existing docs tool pattern first, then I’ll wire in the symmetry-specific logic and update the main docs sidebar entry.
I’ve confirmed the docs set and `rg` isn’t available here, so I’m switching to native PowerShell reads and pulling the existing tool pages that match the desired layout and interactions.
I’ve got the structure reference now. Next I’m pulling the tail of the existing tool page so the new page matches its footer/script conventions instead of inventing a parallel pattern.

🏝️ Maximum Independent Set Finder Find largest independent vertex sets with greedy, exact & complement-clique algorithms

An independent set S of a graph G is a set of vertices where no two are adjacent. The Maximum Independent Set (MIS) is the largest such set — its size is denoted α(G). Finding it is NP-hard, but this tool lets you visualize three different approaches step by step.

—
α(G) Found
0
Vertices
0
Edges
—
Density
0
Steps
In independent set
Current candidate
Eliminated
Available

Presets

Algorithm

Custom Input

Step History

Theorems

α(G) + β(G) = n — Gallai's theorem (α = independence #, β = vertex cover #)
α(G) = ω(Ḡ) — independence # = clique # of complement
Run an algorithm to see computed values.

Export

GraphVisual

Documentation
Getting Started
🏠Overview 🚀Setup Guide
Tools
🔍Graph Explorer 🧭Path Finder 🔎Query Playground 🎲Graph Generator 📈Statistics 🔢Adjacency Matrix 🎨Graph Coloring ✏️Graph Editor 🔍Community Detection 🌲MST Visualizer 🌊Network Flow 🚶Random Walk 🔄Isomorphism ⚖️Bipartite Checker 🌉Bridges & APs 📐Planarity 🛡️Resilience 📋Topo Sort 🔗Max Clique 🚨Anomaly Detector 👑Dominating Set
Architecture
🏗️Architecture 🗄️Database Schema
Reference
📖API Reference 🍳Cookbook

👑 Dominating Set Finder Find minimum dominating sets with greedy & exact algorithms

A dominating set S of a graph G is a subset of vertices such that every vertex is either in S or adjacent to at least one vertex in S. Finding a minimum dominating set is NP-hard, but greedy approximation works well in practice. This tool lets you visualize both approaches step by step.

0
Nodes
0
Edges
—
Dom. Set Size
—
γ(G) / n
0
Steps
In dominating set
Dominated (covered)
Uncovered
Current candidate

Presets

Algorithm

Custom Input

Step History

No steps yet — run an algorithm

About Dominating Sets

📡

Wireless Networks

Dominating sets model optimal placement of relay nodes — every device is within one hop of a relay.

🏥

Facility Location

Place facilities (hospitals, fire stations) so every community is adjacent to at least one.

🔒

Network Monitoring

Position sensors to observe all network links — a dominating set monitors every neighbor.

🧬

Biological Networks

Identify essential proteins in PPI networks: a dominating set covers all interaction partners.

Built by Saurav Bhattacharya • GitHub • Licensed under MIT

📊 GraphVisual Community evolution visualization for student social networks

🚀 Get Started 📖 API Reference 🍳Cookbook ⭐ GitHub

What is GraphVisual?

GraphVisual is a Java desktop application for studying community evolution in student populations using Bluetooth proximity data. It processes meeting records from a PostgreSQL database, classifies social relationships, and renders interactive graph visualizations with timeline playback.

Built for research on social network analysis — specifically understanding how communities form, dissolve, and evolve over time in university settings.

Features

🎨

Interactive Visualization

Drag, zoom, and rotate graph nodes using JUNG's graph mouse. Explore community structures with cluster-based layouts.

⏱️

Timeline Playback

Animate community graphs across 92 days with play/pause/stop controls. Watch relationships form and dissolve.

🏷️

5 Relationship Types

Friends, classmates, study groups, familiar strangers, and strangers — each color-coded on the graph.

🎛️

Adjustable Thresholds

Tune meeting duration and frequency thresholds per relationship type in real-time.

📊

Network Statistics

Real-time metrics: node/edge counts, density, degree stats, hub identification, centrality analysis.

🔬

Community Detection

Connected-component analysis with modularity scoring, community density, and dominant edge-type breakdowns.

🧬

Community Evolution Tracker

Watch communities form, merge, split, and dissolve over time with autonomous event detection and proactive fragility warnings.

🧭

Shortest Path Finder

BFS (hop-count) and Dijkstra (weight-optimal) path finding between any two nodes.

💾

GraphML Export

Export graphs to standard GraphML format for Gephi, Cytoscape, NetworkX, and other tools.

🦠

Diffusion Simulator

Simulate epidemic and information spreading on networks with SIR, SIS, and Independent Cascade models. Proactive containment recommendations.

🏝️

Independent Set Finder

Find maximum independent sets with greedy, exact backtracking, and complement-clique algorithms. Step-by-step animation with Gallai theorem verification.

Relationship Classification

TypeColorLocationDurationFrequency
FriendsGreenPublic areas> 10 min≥ 2/day
ClassmatesBlueClassrooms> 30 min≥ 1/day
Study GroupsOrangeClassrooms> 20 min≤ 1/day
Familiar StrangersGrayPublic/paths< 2 min> 1/day
StrangersRedPublic/paths< 2 min< 2/day

All thresholds are adjustable at runtime via the Category Panel sliders.

Quick Start

# Clone
git clone https://github.com/sauravbhattacharya001/GraphVisual.git
cd GraphVisual

# Configure database
export DB_HOST=localhost DB_USER=your_user DB_PASS=your_pass

# Build with Maven
mvn initialize -P install-local-deps
mvn package -B

# Run
java -jar target/graphvisual-1.1.0-all.jar
See the full setup guide for detailed instructions including Docker, Ant builds, and data pipeline setup.

Tech Stack

TechnologyPurpose
Java 11+Application language
JUNG 2.0.1Graph data structures and visualization
Java SwingDesktop GUI framework
PostgreSQLMeeting and Bluetooth event storage
MavenBuild and dependency management
JUnit 4Unit testing
GitHub ActionsCI/CD, CodeQL, Pages deployment

Documentation

🚀

Setup Guide

Installation, configuration, building, running the data pipeline, Docker deployment.
🏗️

Architecture

Project structure, data pipeline, algorithm details, component design.
🗄️

Database Schema

PostgreSQL table schemas, query patterns, data pipeline ordering.
📖

API Reference

Detailed class and method documentation for all public APIs.

Built by Saurav Bhattacharya • GitHub • Licensed under MIT

📖API Reference 🍳Cookbook

🔄 Graph Isomorphism Checker Determine if two graphs are structurally identical

Draw or type two graphs and check whether they are isomorphic — structurally identical up to vertex relabeling. If isomorphic, the tool shows a valid vertex mapping between them.

Presets:
Draw mode: Click canvas to add nodes. Click two nodes sequentially to add an edge. Right-click a node to delete it.
Edge list mode: Enter edges as pairs like 0-1, 1-2, 2-3 (one graph per box).

Graph A

Graph B

Graph A
0V / 0E
Graph B
0V / 0E
Degree Sequence A
—

🔄 Graph Isomorphism Checker Determine if two graphs are structurally identical

Draw or type two graphs and check whether they are isomorphic — structurally identical up to vertex relabeling. If isomorphic, the tool shows a valid vertex mapping between them.

Presets:
Draw mode: Click canvas to add nodes. Click two nodes sequentially to add an edge. Right-click a node to delete it.
Edge list mode: Enter edges as pairs like 0-1, 1-2, 2-3 (one graph per box).
